body.u: Reflecting back on my life to me the best time was that when I was at school. I now wish I had studied harder than I did. I stayed on for a year ayat my last school and left at the age of 17. Sometimes I wonder why I took that extra year, look at the job I ended up with, a shop assistant in Woolworths. I worked there for about twenty years going through the near on same routine day in and day out except for the odd exceptional days when there was the Christmas rush. At first I was poorly payedpaid, I earned £18 a week. But slowly the wage in my pay packet went up and the most I ever earned there was £38. I left the job once because of having the children but kindly a place was kept for me.

I married at 23 and have two children, wewho are now grown up and have children of their own. When I was younger I used to hate the idea of becoming old and becoming a grandmother but to look at my grandchildren now it seems all worth whileworthwile.

At 38 I left Woolworths for good to be a housewife and to look after my second child. There are ten years between my first and second child. At the age of 50 I got a part time job of working in a small shop which I worked in full time 2 years later. The job payedpaid fair. I found this job more boring than the first, there were less customers, but those who did come to shop there I got to know fairly well, unlike the job at Woolworths where you hardly got to know anyone, but you hardly had the time to usually.

At 60 I retired. Old age seemed to be quiet and things that needed to be done were done with time to spare. I always wonder why I moaned about school because I had longer holiday time then and more time to enjoy myself.
